seoTitle: Close or Stop Accepting Responses in Microsoft Forms
metaDescription: Stop a Microsoft Forms survey or quiz from accepting new responses, show a custom closing message, or schedule an automatic cutoff date.
intro: Ending a survey or quiz shouldn't mean deleting your work or leaving respondents staring at a broken link. Microsoft Forms lets you toggle off response collection instantly, show a custom message to late arrivals, or schedule an automatic end date — all without touching your question layout.

steps.heading: Open form settings
steps.body: Click the three-dot More Form Settings icon and select Settings from the dropdown.

steps.heading: Turn off response collection
steps.body: In the options for responses section, uncheck Accept responses to immediately stop new submissions.

steps.heading: Add a custom closing message
steps.body: Use the newly revealed text field to write a message explaining why the form is no longer accepting entries.

steps.heading: Or schedule an automatic end date
steps.body: Toggle on the End Date option and use the calendar and time picker to set a future closing time instead of stopping manually.

steps.heading: Reopen it later if needed
steps.body: Return to the same settings panel and recheck Accept responses to bring the form back online whenever you're ready.

faq.question: Do closed forms still keep their existing responses?
faq.answer: Yes, closing a form only stops new submissions — all previously collected responses remain intact.

faq.question: Can respondents see why the form is closed?
faq.answer: Yes, you can write a custom message that displays to anyone who clicks the link after you've stopped accepting responses.

faq.question: Is there a way to schedule closing instead of doing it manually?
faq.answer: Yes, the End Date option lets you set an automatic cutoff date and time in advance.

faq.question: Can I reopen a form after closing it?
faq.answer: Yes, simply rechecking the Accept responses box reactivates the form for new submissions.
